title: Analyse a Royal Commission
description: Trace a royal commission or major inquiry from trigger through evidence, findings, recommendations, and actual reform.
whyItMatters: It teaches the difference between public inquiry, public attention, and real institutional change.

steps.title: Select a commission or inquiry
steps.description: Choose a major Australian case with published findings and some public aftermath.

steps.title: Map the trigger and scope
steps.description: Explain why the issue escalated and what the terms of reference covered.

steps.title: Summarise the findings
steps.description: Identify the core failures, the recommendations, and the institutions implicated.

steps.title: Judge what changed
steps.description: Separate symbolic response from actual reform.

deliverables: A short evaluation of what changed and what did not

rubric.description: Does the analysis distinguish investigation from reform?
Does it clearly separate recommendations from implemented change?
